name: United Kingdom

visa_free.notes: Irish citizens do not need a visa or ETA to enter the UK under the Common Travel Area arrangement. They have the right to live, work and study in the UK indefinitely.
Holders of a British overseas territories citizen passport do not need an ETA or visa.
Those travelling with a British National (Overseas) passport do not need an ETA or visa.

visa_policy.notes: The UK operates a three-tier system: (1) British and Irish citizens (and certain British passport types) have free movement under the Common Travel Area; (2) ~86 nationalities require an Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA, £20, up to 6 months); (3) ~130+ nationalities on the Visa National List require a Standard Visitor visa. The Visa National List (Appendix Visitor) is the authoritative source for who needs a visa. ETA mandatory enforcement began 25 February 2026. The UK does not offer 'visa on arrival' or 'e-visa' for tourists - only ETA and pre-travel visas. Recent VNL additions (April 2026): Nicaragua, St Lucia, Nauru, Trinidad and Tobago, and Botswana were removed from the ETA list and added to the Visa National List. Nationals on the visa national list include: Afghanistan, Albania, Algeria, Angola, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bangladesh, Belarus, Benin, Bhutan, Bolivia, Bosnia Herzegovina, Botswana, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cambodia, Cameroon, Cape Verde, Central African Republic, Chad, China, Colombia, Comoros, Congo, Cote d'Ivoire, Cuba, DRC, Djibouti, Dominica, Dominican Republic, Ecuador, Egypt, El Salvador, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Fiji, Gabon, Gambia, Georgia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Haiti, Honduras, India, Indonesia, Iran, Iraq, Jamaica, Jordan, Kazakhstan, Kenya, North Korea, Kosovo, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Lebanon, Lesotho, Liberia, Libya, Madagascar, Malawi, Mali, Mauritania, Moldova, Mongolia, Montenegro, Morocco, Mozambique, Myanmar, Namibia, Nauru, Nepal, Nicaragua, Niger, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Pakistan, Palestine, Philippines, Russia, Rwanda, Sao Tome and Principe, Senegal, Serbia, Sierra Leone, Somalia, South Africa, South Sudan, Sri Lanka, St Lucia, Sudan, Suriname, Syria, Taiwan (without ID card number), Tajikistan, Tanzania, Thailand, Timor-Leste, Togo, Trinidad and Tobago, Tunisia, Turkey, Turkmenistan, Uganda, Ukraine, Uzbekistan, Vanuatu, Venezuela, Vietnam, Yemen, Zambia, Zimbabwe.

conditional_access.conditions: TWOV (Transit Without Visa) scheme — landside transit only. Applicant must: (a) arrive and depart by air; (b) be genuinely in transit to another country on a reasonable transit route; (c) not access public funds/medical treatment/work/study in the UK; (d) intend and be able to leave the UK before 23:59 on the day after arrival; (e) have a confirmed onward flight departing before 23:59 the next day; (f) be assured entry to their destination. Must be travelling to or from (or on part of a reasonable journey to or from) the USA. Physical visa only — electronic visas (including printed versions) not accepted. Note: Syrian nationals holding a B1 or B2 US visa are EXCLUDED from this rule (TWOV 4). Source: Annex A, Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

TWOV (Transit Without Visa) scheme — landside transit only. Must be travelling to or from (or on part of a reasonable journey to or from) Canada. All TWOV 2 base requirements apply: arrive/depart by air, genuinely in transit, no work/study/public funds, leave before 23:59 next day, confirmed onward flight, assured entry to destination. Physical visa only — electronic visas not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(a)/(b), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

TWOV (Transit Without Visa) scheme — landside transit only. Must be travelling to or from (or on part of a reasonable journey to or from) Australia. All TWOV 2 base requirements apply: arrive/depart by air, genuinely in transit, no work/study/public funds, leave before 23:59 next day, confirmed onward flight, assured entry to destination. Physical visa only — electronic visas not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(a)/(b), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

TWOV (Transit Without Visa) scheme — landside transit only. Must be travelling to or from (or on part of a reasonable journey to or from) New Zealand. All TWOV 2 base requirements apply: arrive/depart by air, genuinely in transit, no work/study/public funds, leave before 23:59 next day, confirmed onward flight, assured entry to destination. Physical visa only — electronic visas not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(a)/(b), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

credential.subtype: I-551 Green Card (issued on or after 21 April 1998)

conditional_access.conditions: TWOV scheme — landside transit only. Holder must present: a valid USA I-551 Permanent Resident Card issued on or after 21 April 1998; OR a valid USA I-551 temporary immigrant visa (wet-ink stamp version NOT accepted); OR an expired I-551 card (issued on/after 21 April 1998) accompanied by a valid I-797 letter authorising extension of permanent residency; OR a valid standalone US immigration Form 155A/155B attached to a sealed brown envelope. All TWOV 2 base requirements apply. Physical documents only — electronic versions not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(d)/(e)/(f)/(g), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

TWOV scheme — landside transit only. Must hold a valid Canadian Permanent Resident Card issued after 28 June 2002 (citizenship cards not accepted). No requirement to travel to/from Canada for PR card holders (unlike visa holders). All TWOV 2 base requirements apply. Physical documents only — electronic versions not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(c)(ii), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

TWOV scheme — landside transit only. Must hold a valid Australian Permanent Resident Visa. No requirement to travel to/from Australia for PR holders (unlike visa holders). All TWOV 2 base requirements apply. Physical documents only — electronic versions not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(c)(i), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

TWOV scheme — landside transit only. Must hold a valid New Zealand Permanent Resident Visa. No requirement to travel to/from New Zealand for PR holders (unlike visa holders). All TWOV 2 base requirements apply. Physical documents only — electronic versions not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(c)(iii), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

credential.subtype: valid common format residence permit (EEA state or Switzerland, pursuant to Council Regulation (EC) No. 1030/2002)

conditional_access.conditions: TWOV scheme — landside transit only. Must hold a valid common format residence permit issued by an EEA state or Switzerland pursuant to Council Regulation (EC) No. 1030/2002. All TWOV 2 base requirements apply. Physical documents only — electronic versions not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(h), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

credential.subtype: valid uniform format category D visa (EEA state or Switzerland)

conditional_access.conditions: TWOV scheme — landside transit only. Must hold a valid uniform format Category D (national/long-stay) visa for entry to an EEA state or Switzerland. All TWOV 2 base requirements apply. Physical documents only — electronic versions not accepted. Source: TWOV 3(i), Home Office Transit Guidance v9, 25 March 2026.

Airside transit only without a visa. Applies to People's Republic of China nationals holding a Diplomatic or Service passport. Footnote 1 in UK Visa Requirements for International Carriers (April 2026). Landside transit and standard visitor entry still requires a visa.

Transit (airside and landside) without a visa. Applies to Indian nationals holding a Diplomatic or Official passport. Footnote 3 in UK Visa Requirements for International Carriers (April 2026) — note: unlike China/Vietnam (airside only), India is listed as transit without visa (no airside qualifier), indicating both airside and landside transit are permitted. Standard visitor entry still requires a visa.

Airside transit only without a visa. Applies to Vietnamese nationals holding a Diplomatic or Official passport. Footnote 5 in UK Visa Requirements for International Carriers (April 2026). Landside transit and standard visitor entry still requires a visa.

Airside transit only without a visa. Applies to Turkish nationals holding a Diplomatic passport. Footnote 7 in UK Visa Requirements for International Carriers (April 2026). Also applies to South Africa (same footnote 7). Landside transit and standard visitor entry still requires a visa.

Airside transit only without a visa. Applies to South African nationals holding a Diplomatic passport. Footnote 7 in UK Visa Requirements for International Carriers (April 2026). Landside transit and standard visitor entry still requires a visa.

Full visitor entry without a visa (not transit-only). Immigration Rules Appendix Visitor: Visa National List VN 2.2(d): 'people who hold a Service, Temporary Service or Diplomatic passport issued by the Holy See' do not need a visit visa before travelling to the UK as a Visitor. Standard 6-month visitor conditions apply.

cbi.notes: The UK does not offer a Citizenship by Investment (CBI) program. The previous Tier 1 (Investor) visa was a residency-by-investment route (not citizenship) and was closed to new applications on 17 February 2022. Citizenship is obtained through naturalisation after holding Indefinite Leave to Remain, not via a direct investment-to-citizenship route.

programs.name: Tier 1 (Investor) Visa - CLOSED
programs.notes: This route is CLOSED to new applications as of 17 February 2022. Existing holders may still extend or apply for ILR. Previously required £2 million investment in UK government bonds, share capital or loan capital in active UK companies. Not a new route - listed for completeness only.

programs.name: Global Talent Visa
programs.eligibility: Leaders or potential leaders in academia/research, arts and culture, or digital technology. Must be endorsed by an approved UK body or have won an eligible prestigious prize. Minimum age 18.
programs.processing_time: 3 weeks (outside UK); 8 weeks (inside UK). Fast-track endorsement 2 weeks for eligible academic/research roles.
programs.notes: Allows work in the UK for up to 5 years at a time, renewable. Path to Indefinite Leave to Remain (ILR) after 3-5 years depending on field and track. Application fee: £766 (or £561 + £205 endorsement route). Healthcare surcharge approx. £1,035/year. No need for employer sponsorship. Three fast-track endorsement processes available via British Academy, Royal Society, Royal Academy of Engineering, and UKRI.

programs.name: Innovator Founder Visa
programs.eligibility: Individuals wanting to set up a new, innovative, viable and scalable business in the UK. Must obtain endorsement from an approved body. English language proficiency required. Business idea must be original.
programs.notes: No minimum investment amount specified by government. Visa lasts up to 3 years, renewable. Path to ILR (settlement) after 3 years. Application fee: £1,357 (outside UK) or £1,693 (extension/switch). Endorsement assessment fee: £1,000. Minimum 2 endorsing body meetings at £500 each.

programs.name: Skilled Worker Visa
programs.eligibility: Must have a confirmed job offer from a UK Home Office-approved employer, certificate of sponsorship, eligible occupation, meet minimum salary requirements, English language proficiency.
programs.notes: Visa lasts up to 5 years, renewable. Path to ILR after 5 years. Faster decisions available for additional fee. Most common route for skilled workers.

agreements: Common Travel Area (with Ireland, Jersey, Guernsey, Isle of Man)

validation_notes: ADDED Malta (MLT) to eta list — was erroneously omitted from prior record; confirmed on Appendix ETA National List (2 April 2025 cohort) and the gov.uk ETA eligibility checker page.
UPDATED US ETA note: changed 'ETA mandatory from 25 February 2026' to 'ETA mandatory enforcement began 25 February 2026' to reflect enforcement is now in force (past tense, as of June 2026).

UPDATED overall notes: corrected ETA country count from ~85 to ~86 and noted recent VNL additions (Nicaragua, St Lucia, Nauru, Trinidad and Tobago, Botswana moved from ETA to VNL). These countries were correctly absent from the eta array in the prior record but the note count was off.
VERIFIED: All other ETA-eligible countries in the file match the Appendix ETA National List (Immigration Rules) as of June 2026.
VERIFIED: Visa National List countries in the notes match the Appendix Visitor: Visa National List (updated 29 April 2026).
VERIFIED: conditional_access TWOV rules match Home Office Transit Guidance v9 (25 March 2026).
VERIFIED: Diplomatic/official passport exemptions match UK Visa Requirements for Carriers (April 2026) footnotes.
VERIFIED: CBI confirmed closed; Tier 1 Investor visa confirmed closed to new applications since 17 February 2022.
VERIFIED: ETA fee confirmed £20 (increased from £10 prior to June 2025).
gaps: The visa national list (countries requiring a visa) is confirmed from the official Immigration Rules Appendix Visitor but individual max_stay_days for visa-required countries are not captured — all nationalities on the visa national list require a Standard Visitor visa and are not recorded individually in the visa_free/eta arrays.
Exact salary thresholds for the Skilled Worker visa are not captured (require checking occupation-specific tables on gov.uk).

The Tier 1 (Investor) visa is closed; no active residency-by-investment program currently exists in the UK. The High Potential Individual (HPI) visa and Scale-up visa are also available fast-track routes but were not fully detailed in this research pass.
Taiwan note: Taiwan nationals with a passport that does NOT contain an ID card number are on the visa national list and require a visa; those WITH an ID card number are ETA eligible.
The UK's ETA full country list was confirmed as approximately 86 nationalities from the Appendix ETA National List (Immigration Rules) and the gov.uk ETA guidance page (June 2026).

OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder: No official UK government source (gov.uk, Home Office) was found granting any visa exemption or special status to OCI cardholders for entry into the UK. Indian nationals (including OCI holders) on ordinary passports are on the Visa National List and require a UK visitor visa.

TWOV rules apply to transit only (arrival and departure by air, max stay 23:59 next day, no public funds/work/study). They are NOT general entry rules. Electronic/printed e-visa copies are explicitly excluded — physical visas/PR cards only.
Syria-US visa exclusion: Syrian nationals holding a B1 or B2 category US visa are specifically excluded from TWOV 3(a) and (b) per TWOV 4.

Diplomatic passport waivers in conditional_access reflect footnotes in the April 2026 UK Visa Requirements for Carriers document. Exact bilateral agreement dates are not published in UK immigration rules for these specific exemptions.

outbound_official.notes: The UK government (FCDO / Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office) does not publish a single consolidated outbound visa-free travel list for British ordinary passport holders — there is no analogue to India's mea.gov.in/vffin page. The FCDO publishes 226 individual country-by-country foreign travel advice pages at gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice, each containing an 'Entry requirements' section with specific guidance for British citizens. A dedicated Schengen/EU page (gov.uk/travel-to-eu-schengen-area) states British citizens do not need a visa for short trips (up to 90 days in 180) but this is not a comprehensive outbound visa matrix. Rely on destination-side data and individual FCDO country pages for authoritative outbound travel requirements.

visa_types.name: Standard Visitor Visa
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, visiting family/friends, business meetings, short courses
visa_types.notes: Apply online at gov.uk. Most applicants will be asked to attend a visa application centre. Fee is approximately £115.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Studying a course at a licensed UK student sponsor for longer than 6 months
visa_types.notes: Requires a Confirmation of Acceptance for Studies (CAS) from a UK Visas and Immigration-licensed sponsor. Fee approximately £363–£490 depending on course length.

visa_types.name: Skilled Worker Visa
visa_types.purpose: Working in an eligible skilled job with a UK employer
visa_types.notes: Requires a job offer from a UK licensed sponsor paying at least the required salary threshold. Fee approximately £719 for up to 3 years.

visa_types.name: Youth Mobility Scheme (Working Holiday)
visa_types.purpose: Living and working in the UK for up to 2 years (age 18–30, select nationalities)
visa_types.notes: Available to nationals of Australia, Canada, New Zealand, Japan, South Korea, Hong Kong, Taiwan, and others via bilateral agreements. Fee approximately £259.

visa_types.name: Innovator Founder Visa
visa_types.purpose: Setting up or running a business in the UK that is innovative, viable, and scalable
visa_types.notes: Requires endorsement from a UKVI-approved endorsing body. No minimum investment amount specified. Fee approximately £1,191.
